channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Angular - NgRx Facade Design Pattern
item.description: In this article I'll show how I use the Facade Design Pattern on my angular projects. Facade Design Pattern I read about this pattern from this website: facade This is a structural design pattern...

item.title: Arquitectura Hexagonal con Spring Boot y Angular: lo que aprendí aplicándola en producción
item.description: La mayoría de los posts sobre arquitectura hexagonal hablan de mantenibilidad y testabilidad. Eso está bien, pero hay una dimensión que casi nadie menciona: la arquitectura hexagonal es, por...

item.title: The Unexpected Benefits of Knowledge Hoarding: What Two Years with Papers Taught Me About Information Addiction
item.description: The Unexpected Benefits of Knowledge Hoarding: What Two Years with Papers Taught Me About Information Addiction Honestly, I never thought I'd become this person. The one who has 12,847 articles...

item.title: AgentCore - The Foundation of Agentic AI Systems
item.description: What is AgentCore? AgentCore is a conceptual and architectural backbone for building AI agents that can operate independently over time. It combines several critical capabilities into a cohesive...

item.title: How I got out of dependency hell with Docker (SpiderFoot OSINT lab)
item.description: I wanted to try SpiderFoot – an OSINT tool. Thought it would be a simple pip install. Nope. It crashed on lxml. Something about headers and versions. Classic dependency hell on Arch. I didn't want...

item.title: How to Add Link Previews to Any App in One Line of Code
item.description: Ever needed to show a preview of a PDF, document, or website in your app? Setting up Puppeteer, LibreOffice, and ffmpeg is painful. Here's a simpler way. The problem You have a list of file URLs —...

item.title: The 6 Git Hooks I Copy Into Every New Repo
item.description: Every new repo I start gets the same six git hooks copied in before the first commit lands Pre-commit lint and type-check runs catch noisy mistakes before they hit CI, saving 30 to 60 seconds per...

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down and will be succeeded by John Ternus, currently Apple's senior vice president of hardware engineering. Ternus will take over as CEO on September 1st, 2026. Cook...

item.title: Here’s how Amazon’s price fixing allegedly drove up prices everywhere
item.description: On Monday, California Attorney General Rob Bonta revealed the evidence of alleged price-fixing by Amazon. The state filed a request to the Supreme Court in February for a preliminary injunction to...

item.title: Tim Cook to become Apple Executive Chairman
item.description: Article URL: https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2026/04/tim-cook-to-become-apple-executive-chairman-john-ternus-to-become-apple-ceo/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47840219 Points:...

item.description: Article URL: https://www.macrumors.com/2026/04/20/tim-cook-stepping-down/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47840165 Points: 19 # Comments: 3

item.title: WCAG: Making the internet more accessible
item.description: We at Centro Labs recently finished making LocalMate WCAG 2.2 Level AA Compliant. It's not something that shows up on a demo reel or has any flashy consequences, but it's one of the more...

item.title: Silicon Valley has forgotten what normal people want
item.description: One of the most mortifying things about knowing a lot of techies is listening to them tell me excitedly about some very important discovery that they believe they have made. Recently, I ran into...

item.title: WhatsApp tests ‘Plus’ subscription that adds stickers and more for a few bucks a month
item.description: WhatsApp users are getting some new customization features - for a price. According to WABetaInfo, Meta is rolling out a WhatsApp Plus subscription to "a limited number of users," offering several...

item.title: Japanese man sentenced to prison for posting spoilers
item.description: Though it's very common for entertainment writers to describe some narrative elements of the films and shows that they're covering, a Japanese court has determined that the practice can sometimes...

item.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
item.description: This sentence construction ("It's not just this — it's that") has become so common in AI-generated writing that it's no longer just a clue that a piece of writing may be synthetic — it's almost a...

item.title: Google Photos adds subtle touch-up tools for faces
item.description: Google has announced a new collection of touch-up tools for Google Photos' image editor designed to apply subtle enhancements, refinements, or fixes to faces in photos. The tools are gradually...

item.title: Insta360 is putting screens on its next wireless mics to show logos or images
item.description: Insta360's first wireless microphone kit debuted in June with small, lightweight AirTag-sized mics that were easier to wear hidden away. The new Insta360 Mic Pro, which the company is teasing...

item.title: Mercedes’ first all-electric C-Class is its sportiest one yet
item.description: The Mercedes-Benz C-Class, typically a benchmark in luxury compact sedans, now gets an all-important electric variant. The new C 400 4MATIC is built on an 800-volt architecture designed for...

item.title: You can grab a Sonos Era 100 for $85 off thanks to Sonos’ latest refurb sale
item.description: The new Sonos Era 100 SL keeps costs down by dropping the mics, but if you’d rather keep hands-free voice controls, picking up a refurbished, non-SL model is the way to go. Thankfully, Sonos is...

item.title: Deezer says AI song uploads have nearly overtaken human music
item.description: Deezer says it receives nearly 75,000 AI-generated song submissions to its music streaming platform each day, accounting for about 44 percent of all daily uploads, as reported earlier by...

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as...

item.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
item.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

item.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
item.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware....

item.title: Prego Has a Dinner-Conversation-Recording Device, Capisce?
item.description: The pasta-sauce company has partnered with the nonprofit StoryCorps on a device designed to record family conversations around the table and save them for all time.

item.title: The Download: murderous ‘mirror’ bacteria, and Chinese workers fighting AI doubles
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. No one’s sure if synthetic mirror life will kill us all In...

item.title: Why Most AI Deployments Stall After the Demo
item.description: The fastest way to fall in love with an AI tool is to watch the demo. Everything moves quickly. Prompts land cleanly. The system produces impressive outputs in seconds. It feels like the beginning...

item.title: Is “Satoshi Nakamoto” Really Adam Back?
item.description: The New York Times has a long article where the author lays out an impressive array of circumstantial evidence that the inventor of Bitcoin is the cypherpunk Adam Back. I don’t know. The article...

item.title: Tech CEOs Think AI Will Let Them Be Everywhere at Once
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg and Jack Dorsey have different visions for how to use AI for management purposes, but both imagine a system of heightened control.
